chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C.P No. D-3663 of 2021 __________________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s). 1. For orders on Misc. No.15296/2021 2. For orders on Office Objection No.26. 3. For orders on Misc. No.15297/2021 4. For hearing of main case. 10.06.2021 Mr. Abdul Razzaque Solangi, Advocate for the Petitioner. ________________ 1. Granted. 2. Deferred. 3. Granted subject to all just exceptions. 4 The Petitioner has invoked the jurisdiction of this Court under Article 199 of the Constitution impugning 18 First Information Reports (“FIRs”) registered at various Police Stations, wherein her husband stands implicated as one of the accused on allegations of cheating and criminal intimidation. It has been stated that the accused husband of the Petitioner left Pakistan on 09.03.2019, and has been residing at Dubai since, hence the Petitioner has come forward to seek deletion of his name from all those FIRs, and to restrain the registration of further FIR as well as restrain the respondent police functionaries from harassing her in relation to the cases. We have examined the Petition and heard counsel in the matter. The Petitioner lacks locus-standi, with the Petition even otherwise not being maintainable as the relief elicited cannot be advanced through the instant Petition, especially where the only ground raised is that the FIRs are false fabricated and bogus, which is a matter of factual determination. Furthermore, if any harassment is being caused, the Petitioner may invoke the jurisdiction of the Justice of Peace under Section 22 (A) (B) of the Criminal Procedure Code.

The Petitioner lacks locus-standi, with the Petition even otherwise not being maintainable as the relief elicited cannot be advanced through the instant Petition, especially where the only ground raised is that the FIRs are false fabricated and bogus, which is a matter of factual determination. Furthermore, if any harassment is being caused, the Petitioner may invoke the jurisdiction of the Justice of Peace under Section 22 (A) (B) of the Criminal Procedure Code. That being so, the Petition stands dismissed accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE TariqAli/PA
